ELO Card Ratings with Per-Act Win Rates

🏆 Win% columns show how often runs were won when this card was first picked in that act. Min 3 ELO games required. Act columns require ≥2 picks. Advanced: ELO ± column shows Glicko-2 confidence interval width (2×RD).

📖 Dashboard Help & Definitions

🔧 Global Filters (Top of Dashboard)

All filters apply to every tab at once. Adjust them at the top of the page to focus your analysis.

FilterWhat it does
CharacterRestrict all data to runs where you played that character (Ironclad, Silent, Defect, Necrobinder, Regent, or All).
Min AscensionSlider from 0–10. Only runs at this ascension level or higher are included. E.g. setting 5 shows Asc 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10 combined.
OutcomeShow only Wins, only Losses, or All runs.
ModeFilter by player count — 1P (solo), 2P, 3P, 4P, or All. All stats are always based on your own character's data, regardless of mode.

📊 Run Group

Overview of your runs and difficulty progression.

📊 Overview

High-level summary of your runs under the current filters.

  • Win Rate by Ascension: Line chart showing how your win rate changes across difficulty levels 0–10.
  • Win Rate by Character: Bar chart showing overall win rate for each character.
  • Deck Size Targets: The average final deck size in winning runs — a guide for how many cards to aim for at each ascension.

📈 Ascension

Drills into difficulty progression from Ascension 0 to 10.

  • Difficulty Curve: Win rate line chart per ascension level — spots which levels are hardest.
  • Per-Ascension Table: Runs played, wins, avg deck size, avg relics, and the deadliest enemy at each level.

📋 Runs

A full history of every run that matches the current filters, plus a cumulative win-rate chart below showing your rolling win rate over time with a Kalman-smoothed trend line.

ColumnMeaning
DateLocal date the run started.
CharacterThe character you played.
AscAscension level (0–10).
OutcomeWin (green) or Loss (red).
Killed ByEnemy or event that ended the run. "-" for wins.
CardsFinal deck size.
RelicsNumber of relics in your final loadout.
Total Damage TakenSum of all HP lost across every combat encounter in the run (includes all fights, not just losses).
Act ReachedHighest act (1, 2, or 3) you reached — derived from your last recorded combat (not map floor #, but act #).
DurationTotal real-time length of the run.
Players1P = solo, 2P/3P/4P = multiplayer.
AlliesMultiplayer only — characters your teammates played.

🃏 Deck Group

Analysis of the cards, relics, and potions that make up your deck.

🃏 Cards

Analytics for every non-starter card you've been offered. Starter cards (Strike, Defend, Bash, Neutralize, Zap, etc.), Curses, and Status cards are excluded — they aren't player choices.

MetricMeaning
Pick Rate% of runs where this card ended up in your final deck.
Skip Rate% of times you were offered this card and did not take it.
Win %Win rate of all runs that included this card in the final deck.
Deck %On average, what fraction of your final deck was this card (including duplicates). (e.g. if 3 copies in 30-card deck = 10%)
Act 1/2/3 Win %Win rate specifically when this card was first picked in that act.

✨ Relics

Analytics for every non-starter relic. Starting relics (Burning Blood, Ring of the Snake, Cracked Core, Bound Phylactery, Divine Right) are excluded.

MetricMeaning
Pick Rate% of runs where this relic was in your final loadout.
Win %Win rate of all runs where you had this relic.
ELORelic strength estimate versus a 1500 baseline, updated by run outcomes whenever this relic was present.

🧪 Potions

Lifecycle counts for every potion type across all filtered runs. A high Used/Picked ratio indicates a potion you reliably consume rather than carry and discard.

MetricMeaning
OfferedTotal times this potion appeared as a reward option.
PickedTimes you chose it from a reward screen.
UsedTimes you actually consumed it in combat.
DiscardedTimes you dropped it to free a slot.

🔗 Synergies

Shows card-to-card and relic-to-relic pairings that frequently appear together in winning runs. The bubble chart plots:

  • X-axis: How often the pair appears together (frequency).
  • Y-axis: Win rate when that pair is present (performance).
  • Bubble size: Number of games with the pair — larger bubbles = more data.

Focus on combos with both high frequency and high win rate. Small bubbles in the top-left are "hidden gems" — rare but strong synergies worth trying.

⚔️ Combat Group

Analysis of encounters, performance patterns, and successful builds.

⚔️ Encounters

Stats for every enemy type you've faced, including which acts they appear in.

MetricMeaning
Survivor Rate% of fights against this enemy where you survived (HP > 0 after combat).
Avg DamageAverage HP lost in a single fight against this enemy.
Times Ended RunHow many times this enemy was your last fight in a losing run — your personal nemesis.
Act BreakdownSurvival rates split by which act the fight occurred in — see if you struggle more early or late.

🔥 Heatmap

A Character × Ascension grid showing your win rate per cell. Color scale adapts per character: Red (low win rate) → that character's primary color → Green (high win rate). Win rate % is printed inside each cell for easy reference. Useful for spotting which character/ascension combos are your strongest and which need work.

🎭 Builds

Extracts and displays the most common card and relic combinations found in your winning runs, grouped by character and ascension. This shows you the archetypal "winning recipes" at each difficulty level — useful as a reference for deckbuilding decisions during a run. Cards are sorted by frequency, so you see your most reliable picks first.

🗺️ Map Group

Analysis of map encounters and act-start blessings.

🗺️ Floors

Per-floor danger statistics. Each row represents a floor type in a specific act.

ColumnMeaning
Floor TypeWeak (easy monster), Normal (standard monster), Elite, Boss, Event, Rest, Shop, Treasure, or Ancient.
ActThe named act (e.g. Overgrowth, Hive, Glory) — useful for comparing acts across game versions.
Act IndexPosition in the run: 1 = first act, 2 = second, 3 = third. (numbers not floor numbers)
Death %% of runs that ended on this floor type (your last floor before losing). Highlighted gold ≥ 3%, red ≥ 10%.

🌟 Ancients

Blessings offered by the ancient at the start of each act (Neow for Act 1, different ancients for Acts 2 & 3). Only shows stats for blessings you actually chose — skipped blessings don't affect their metrics.

MetricMeaning
ELOBlessing strength estimate. Updated only when you chose this blessing.
Pick %How often you chose this blessing when it was in the offered pool.
Win %Win rate of runs where you took this blessing.

Tip: Use the Act filter (1/2/3) to compare blessing quality within each individual ancient's pool.

🏆 ELO Ratings

Card strength rankings per character. Data is ordered by current ELO rating. The ELO system uses a dynamic K-factor (starts at 48, drops to 32 then 24 as more data accumulates), scaled by ascension difficulty, and adjusted based on how cleanly you won (damage taken).

ColumnMeaning
ELOCurrent strength rating. 1500 = baseline average.
±CIConfidence interval around the ELO (how sure we are of this rating) — shows uncertainty. Wider intervals = fewer games played. Displayed as ± 2 × Rating Deviation (RD).
Peak ELOHighest rating this card has ever reached (historical high water mark) — indicates potential even if recent runs brought it down.
Win %Overall win rate across all runs this card appeared in.
Act 1/2/3 Win %Win rate specifically when this card was first picked in that act.
SkippedCount of times you were offered this card but did not take it (offer count, not %). Red highlight = frequently skipped.

ELO Method Selector

The ELO tab includes a toggle between two rating models:

  • Classic ELO: Simple damage-weighted model. Wins with low damage get higher ratings; losses with many encounters get credit for progressing far. Works well for quick assessment.
  • Advanced ELO (Glicko-2): More sophisticated model that also tracks:
    • Act-weighted scoring (dying to Act 3 Boss counts more than early loss).
    • Floor weighting (cards picked late in run get more credit for wins).
    • Confidence intervals (RD value shows certainty — tighter for heavily-played cards).
    • Synergy effects (card ratings adjust based on deck context).

Use Classic for a quick look, Advanced for detailed analysis with confidence bounds.

📥 Export

Download the currently filtered runs as a CSV file. The table preview shows all rows that will be exported. Columns match the Runs tab: Date, Character, Ascension, Outcome, Killed By, Cards, Relics, Total Damage Taken, Act Reached, Duration, Players, Allies. Click any column header to sort before downloading.

ℹ️ General

👥 Multiplayer Runs

In 2P/3P/4P runs, all stats (damage taken, cards, relics, win/loss) reflect your own character only — not your allies'. The Allies column in the Runs tab shows which characters your teammates played. This ensures fair comparison between solo and multiplayer runs.

💡 Quick Tips

  • Click any table column header to sort; click again to reverse the sort direction.
  • Set Min Ascension to 5 to see aggregated stats across all higher difficulties at once.
  • ELO stabilises with more runs — cross-check with Win % and Pick Rate for cards with fewer than 10 appearances.
  • Use the Floors tab with the Act filter to compare how dangerous the same floor type is across different acts.
  • Use the Ancients tab Act filter to compare blessing quality within each individual ancient's pool.
  • Total Damage Taken is the sum of damage taken in every combat encounter across the entire run.
  • Act Reached is calculated from your last recorded combat — a run that died to an event before any fight may show "-".
  • The Kalman smoothed trend line in the Runs tab shows your true skill progression over time, filtering out lucky/unlucky streaks.
